items.title: Russia launched a missile strike on Dnipro, targeting infrastructure. Rescue operations are still underway at the site. All emergency services are involved, helping people with everything they need. Tragically, five people have been confirmed killed as of now. My condolences to their families and loved ones. Twenty-nine people were wounded in the attack. Russia also struck Zaporizhzhia today, targeting an ordinary passenger minibus. Three lives have been lost in this drone attack. My condolences. Six others were wounded, including a child. In Nikopol, the Russians carried out a double-tap strike on first responders and a fire engine. They also attacked energy infrastructure in the Sumy, Odesa, and Chernihiv regions, and launched strikes on Kherson and the Kharkiv region. It is very important to counter this Russian terror. People need greater protection from such horrific attacks. Above all, we need anti-ballistic capabilities. It is essential that Europe is as active as possible in developing its own anti-ballistic defense – its own systems and missiles. The sooner we have more of these capabilities, the more lives we will be able to save.
items.summary:

items.title: Hormuz Traffic Declines as Ship Attacks Spark New Concerns
items.summary: Commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz this weekend persisted at a reduced level as a handful of vessels made open transits. Traffic was still higher than for most of the US-Iran war, but attacks on two ships raised fresh concerns about the ability and willingness of shipowners to traverse the waterway. Alaric Nightingale reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: JPMorgan’s Matejka Is Biggest Europe Stocks Bull With New Target
items.summary: As European stocks were hammered by the Iran war, JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s Mislav Matejka predicted the declines would be short-lived. The strategist is now doubling down, becoming the biggest bull among forecasters tracked by Bloomberg.

items.title: Spain Lifts 2026 Economic-Growth Forecast Despite Iran War
items.summary: Spain will continue to outperform the euro area’s largest economies, with the conflict in Iran expected to have only a limited impact, according to new government forecasts.

items.title: Goldman Backs India 30-Year Bonds as Iran War Impact Contained
items.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. recommends buying India’s 30-year government bonds as inflation expectations ease and lower oil prices reduce fiscal risks.

items.title: Dubai Stocks Set for Best Quarter in a Year as War Premium Ebbs
items.summary: Dubai’s stocks index is set for its best quarter in a year after a truce in the Middle East conflict drew investors back to a market they fled when Iranian missile attacks shattered the city-state’s reputation for stability.

items.summary: As Ukraine brings the war home to Russia, officials hesitate to designate shelters and blast sirens, downplaying the conflict’s consequences with euphemisms.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
items.summary:
The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues.

It follows days of tit-for-tat attacks that risk slowing progress toward restoring traffic through the vital waterway. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.summary:
An anti-narcotics police officer in China who has been working on the frontline without obscuring her identity for the past nine years, incurring more than 10 injuries, has won massive admiration from the mainland public.
Li Xiangli is the only female police station chief in Yunnan province, located in the southwestern part of the country.
